title: My Research
text: Here are my research papers and where they’re published. Most of my work is on AI for wireless and distributed systems. Take a look and get in touch if you want to talk about any of it.

title: Resume
text:
Download My Academic CV - PDF Download My Regular CV - PDF

ProfileEducationExperiencePersonal projectsSkills



I have a BSc in Data Science and Engineering from Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya, with a solid base in CS, math, statistics, and signal processing. I’m now doing a Ph.D. in Computer Engineering and I’m into research that actually moves things forward—especially in ML and AI. I like to get things done and I’m looking to have a real impact in the field.




PhD in Computer Engineering, Northeastern University, Boston
January 2024 - Present
My work is at the intersection of ML, LLMs, distributed training and inference, and edge AI. I care about making models more resilient and robust, and about how we can run AI efficiently on edge and cloud. I also look at how AI can improve telecom—network performance, automation. I’m especially interested in LLMs: attention, knowledge representation, and editing. I want to build systems that are efficient, scalable, and reliable.


BSc degree in Data Science and Engineering, Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya, Barcelona
September 2019 - July 2023
Graduated with a GPA of 8.89/10. Got honors in four courses: Discrete Mathematics and Logic, Mathematical Optimization, Search and Analysis of Information, and Advanced Topics of Data Science.


High School, La Salle Bonanova, Barcelona
September 2017 - June 2019
Graduated with honors; scored 13.556/14 on the university entrance exams (PAU) and got the PAU distinction. Took part in math contests (Pangea, “Fem Matemàtiques”) and did extra math enrichment classes.




Genesys Lab at Northeastern University, BSc thesis development
February 2023 - July 2023
I worked with a research team at the Institute for the Wireless Internet of Things on AI for wireless communications. I designed and implemented AI-based approaches for signal classification. That work got me interested on AI + wireless and I’ve been in that space since.


Abi Global Health, AI Engineer internship
March 2022 - December 2022
Interned at a global tele-health company. I improved monitoring in their MLOps setup and helped the AI Lab build new ML models and improve existing ones.




Datathon/Hackathon participation
2021 - Present
I’ve done a bunch of datathons and hackathons—UPC Datathon and Google Hash Code (twice). They’ve sharpened my problem-solving and teamwork, and I like doing them whenever I can.


BECO, a tool to boost environmentally conscious decisions
February 2022 - June 2022
Built a software solution for Naptilus Tech Lab that used user data to help people reduce their carbon footprint. We shipped “BECO”, an app that used ML to suggest more sustainable options (e.g. eco-friendly places).




I’m strong in problem-solving and critical thinking, and I work well in teams. I pick up new things quickly and I’m comfortable with change. I care about doing good work, I manage my time well, and I stay organized. That’s how I get things done.

title: Miquel Sirera Perelló
text:
Hi, I’m Miquel. I’m doing a Ph.D. focused on Large Language Models (LLMs) and distributed AI—how we train and run these systems across many devices. I like working at the intersection of machine learning and distributed systems and I’m always open to collaboration. If any of this sounds interesting or you have questions, reach out on LinkedIn or at sirera.m@northeastern.edu.

title: Miquel Sirera Perelló
text:
Current projects


Building Resilience on Distributed Large Language Models

We’re extending the JARVIS project—a distributed framework for LLMs that splits model layers across edge devices with limited compute. More in My Research.
JARVIS adds resilience to node failures via peer-to-peer communication and layer redundancy, so the system keeps working when things go wrong. We tested it with Google’s Gemma (2B parameters) on 18 software-defined radios in the NSF Colosseum RF emulator and a 7-node Raspberry Pi testbed.
Right now we’re looking at model changes that handle layer loss better, and at using redundancy in middle layers for faster recovery and more robust distributed runs.


Extension of Communication-Aware DNN Pruning

We’re extending communication-aware DNN pruning: training networks for distributed deployment so they need less communication while staying accurate. We prune and place neurons in CNNs and MLPs so they run well on the edge under mixed network conditions.

Knowledge Editing in Large Language Models

We’re looking at more efficient ways to edit knowledge in LLMs—updating or fixing specific facts and behaviors without full retraining, and without hurting overall performance.

title: Cover Letter — Research Intern, Summer 2026
text:
Miquel Sirera Perelló
PhD Student, Computer Engineering, Northeastern University
sirera.m@northeastern.edu | LinkedIn | Google Scholar

I am writing to apply for the Research Intern position at Fujitsu Research of America for summer 2026. I am a US-based PhD student in Computer Engineering at Northeastern University, and I am very interested in contributing to your team’s work on HCI and AI/computer vision systems with a publication-oriented focus.
My research sits at the intersection of machine learning, AI systems, and practical deployment. I work on large language models and distributed AI—how we train and run these systems across devices—and on making them more resilient and efficient at the edge. I also have direct experience in computer vision and ML for perception: I am a co-author on “Neural Point Processes for Pixel-wise Regression” (AISTATS 2025), where we combined spatial models with neural networks for pixel-wise regression on sparsely annotated images. That work sharpened my interest in building ML/CV systems that are both methodologically sound and usable in real settings, which aligns well with HCI + AI/CV research aimed at publication.
Beyond that, I have built and evaluated AI systems in wireless and distributed settings. I co-authored JARVIS (MILCOM 2024), a distributed LLM framework that runs across edge devices and handles failures via redundancy and peer-to-peer communication—work that involved system design, experimentation, and writing for a top venue. I have also contributed to communication-aware DNN pruning and to projects on knowledge editing in LLMs, and I have industry experience as an AI Engineer intern (ML models and MLOps). I am comfortable moving between theory, implementation, and writing papers, and I am motivated to do research that leads to strong, publishable results.
I would be glad to bring this background to your team’s HCI + AI/CV efforts for summer 2026, to collaborate on concrete research problems, and to work toward a publication. Thank you for considering my application; I look forward to the possibility of discussing how I can contribute to Fujitsu Research of America.
Best regards,
Miquel Sirera Perelló
